title: Gallery Benches
desc: Thousands of UnderWorlders stomp, lean, and shove through these benches. The benches have noticed.

steps.text:
The galleries of the UnderWorld Colosseum are built for crowds that treat seating as a suggestion. Benches are chained to the stone tiers. Rail bars are pinned deep into masonry. Even the step edges are capped with iron where too many stomping feet have learned to wear stone down faster than reason says they should. After last night's main event, section four is loose enough that the seat inspector has closed the row until it can be trusted again.

He slaps the back of the worst bench and listens to it rattle. "Bench chains first if you like heavy work," he says. "Rail pins first if you prefer leaning over a drop while holding tools. Neither task is fun. Both are better than letting the audience test gravity in groups."

options.label: Retension the bench chains along the closed row
Reset the rail pins and brace the gallery edge

steps.text:
The bench chains have not failed. They have simply stretched to the point where a hard stomp from the right crowd can make the whole row answer back. You unhook each run, draw the slack out at the wall ring, and reseat the links so the bench backs pull tight against their anchor points again. It is blunt work, all leverage and iron weight, but the result is immediate.

By the time the last chain is tensioned, the row no longer chatters when the inspector throws his shoulder into it. The sound changes from clatter to solid knock, which is exactly what he wanted. He walks the whole row once, kicking each bench in turn with professional affection. "There," he says. "Now they can shout themselves stupid without taking the seating with them."

options.label: Hook the final chain and reopen the row

steps.text:
The rail pins matter more than the benches because the crowd only remembers the rails when it needs them most. Two in section four have worked half a finger-width loose, enough to make the top bar tremble when leaned on. You drive the pins back into their stone pockets, set fresh wedges around the most worn one, and test the whole run with your weight while the inspector watches the joint lines for movement.

Nothing shifts. That is the only acceptable result. Below you, the arena floor looks farther away than is comfortable, which is perhaps why the rail suddenly feels like a public duty rather than just another repair. The inspector grips the top bar, gives it a savage shake, and nods. "Good. Let the crowd be reckless on its own time."

options.label: Pack the wedges away and leave the edge trustworthy
